cover.title: THE PIONEER
cover.subtitle: The one who carries it all, until they can't anymore.
cover.tagline: You don't fall fast. But once you commit, you carry everything.

profile_scores.caption: High Drive · High Care, Low Expression · Low Stability. Once you commit, you carry everything, but you never let your own state show.

short_summary: You watch people carefully before you ever choose them, and once you do, you carry the whole relationship without saying so.
The exhaustion you've felt before wasn't from loving too hard. It was from never once being on the receiving end.
What you're still learning isn't how to love someone. It's how to let someone love you back.

full_report.headline: THE PIONEER
full_report.intro: You don't fall in love fast. If you choose someone, it's never just attraction. You watch them for a while first, and the decision happens naturally, on its own. This report follows the pattern underneath that: why you keep choosing people who need carrying, what it's cost you, and what actually changes it.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You do not fall in love quickly. When you choose someone, it is rarely just attraction. You watch for a while first, and the decision grows quietly.
People around you often see only the strong version. The one who handles things alone and shows up when it matters. What sits underneath that strength may stay hidden.

When you look at someone, you may see not only who they are now, but who they could become. That ability can be a real strength. It can also make it easier to carry hope long after the relationship has become heavy.
chapters.profile_note: High Drive · High Care, Low Expression · Low Stability. Once you commit, you carry everything, but you never let your own state show.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Keep Repeating
body: This pattern may feel familiar. Someone came into your life needing support, so you stayed. You gave time, energy, and steadiness. When they struggled, you helped hold things together.

Then one day, you may realize you have been carrying most of it alone. It does not mean they were a bad person. The relationship may simply have shifted from being together into managing everything for them.
chapters.highlight: The exhaustion was not only from loving deeply. It came from giving support without feeling it return.

chart.title: THE EXHAUSTION CURVE
chart.description: Energy drains as the relationship goes on, then rises again at the moment of realization.

chapters.title: What You've Gotten Wrong About Yourself

items.truth: You may need people more than you let yourself show. At some point, leaning on someone may have started to feel risky, so handling things alone became familiar.
items.truth_short: You need support too. You may simply be more used to hiding that need.

items.belief: If I love enough, they'll become who I see.
items.truth: The potential you saw may have been real. But potential is not the same as readiness, and love cannot make someone grow before they choose to.
items.truth_short: You may see real potential, but people still grow at their own pace.

items.belief: I got hurt because I gave too much.
items.truth: Giving was not the whole problem. The deeper issue may have been trusting the relationship before enough care, consistency, and responsibility had been shown.
items.truth_short: The issue may not be how much you gave, but whether trust had enough evidence behind it.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: The faces change, but a similar pattern can return. You may be drawn to someone who needs support, direction, or time to become steadier.
Being needed can feel close to being loved. But needing your strength and caring about your whole experience are not the same thing.
chapters.highlight: Someone may need what you give. A caring partner also wants to know how you are doing.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: What you need is not someone who is only impressed by your strength.
What may help is someone who notices when you are tired, checks in before being asked, and does not expect you to stay strong every time.
chapters.action_sentence: In a healthier relationship, support can move toward you too.

chapters.title: Where You Go From Here
body: You already know how to love and support someone.
What you may be learning next is how to let care reach you without turning it into a test, assuming it will fail, or hiding your need until no one can see it.
chapters.transition_note: You've spent a long time being strong for other people. The next step is letting strength and care move both ways.

chapters.title: The Types You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: Someone Who Seems to Need Saving
items.description: They may seem lost, worn down, or unsure of where they are going. You see what they could become. Over time, you may realize you have become their foundation instead of their partner.

items.type_title: Someone Who Is Hard to Read
items.description: They open slowly and reveal little. At first, that can feel intriguing. But mystery and emotional distance are not always the same thing.
They admire how capable you are. But admiration for your strength does not always mean they know how to stay close when you are tired or unsure.
They have talent, ideas, and promise. You may invest in who they could become, while the present version of the relationship keeps asking you to wait.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: Some of these signs may have been there early. Hope can make them easier to explain away.

signal_strength.label: Most of the conversation is about them, and because you're good at listening, it feels natural
When you're together, you think more about how to help than about your own feelings
Your entire schedule, plans, and habits have changed. Theirs barely have
On hard days, you stay quiet because you do not want to add to their load

chapters.core_signal: You notice their difficult days quickly. The question is whether your difficult days receive the same care and attention.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like for You

checklist.title: They show up without being asked.
checklist.description: They notice changes, ask what you need, and follow through with care.

checklist.title: They can handle you when you're not okay.
checklist.description: You do not have to perform strength. They can stay close when you are tired, uncertain, or upset.

checklist.title: They're curious about you, not just impressed by you.
checklist.description: They want to know your inner world, not only admire what you can handle.

checklist.title: Being together makes you lighter, not heavier.
checklist.description: Time together does not leave you carrying more than before.

chapters.title: What Happens In Your Next Relationship (THE PIONEER)

sections.who_breaks_you: You're vulnerable to someone whose needs and potential keep pulling you into the role of supporter. You may feel responsible for keeping them moving, while your own needs remain outside the relationship.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier match is someone with a clear sense of self who does not expect you to carry the whole direction. They can receive your strength without depending on it, and they make room for your uncertainty too.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to someone who needs support or shows visible potential. That does not decide the ending. What matters is whether they take responsibility for their own growth and whether care begins moving back toward you.
sections.next_signs: This time, the pattern may appear in smaller moments. They accept your help easily, but rarely ask how you are doing. Notice whether that changes as the relationship develops.
sections.try_this: Ask for one small form of support before you are exhausted. Say clearly what would help, then notice whether care becomes more mutual over time. Do not use one response as the whole verdict. Look for consistency.
sections.final_line: What matters is whether care, responsibility, and attention begin moving in both directions.

action_plan.summary: You already know how to carry a relationship. What's left is learning to let someone carry part of it with you.
action_items: This week, ask for one small form of support instead of offering first.
When you feel tired, say so before going quiet.
Before investing in someone's potential, look at how they are living and acting now.

closing.message: You've spent years learning how to carry relationships. This report covered why you keep choosing people who need carrying in the first place, and how that changes.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
